evidence: Each frame carries one load-bearing move: frame 1 the fold-down to a dot, frames 4-5 the probe sweep with the score bars as the current work while the candidate arrows anchor above.
The busiest frame (4) holds one amber probe, four periwinkle candidates as a group, and four lilac bars as a group, built up across prior frames rather than dumped at once.
Each arrow is labeled beside it (probe, c1-c4) and each score bar sits under its own c1-c4 label with its numeric readout attached; no legend or key is used.
All on-screen text is labels or numeric readouts (timbre/tempo values, c1-c4, signed scores) attached to visible arrows and bars; no free-floating prose.
The mono numerics and labels are comfortably readable, though the faint half-rendered shapes near center-left in frame 3 are illegible ghosts rather than intended text.
All arrows, labels, and bars sit inside the frame with margin in every frame; nothing is truncated by an edge.
The 'c1' label sits immediately adjacent to the amber probe head in frames 2 and 5-6 but does not overlap it, and no text is stamped across an unrelated line.
The probe is consistently amber (attention), the four candidates periwinkle (observed), and the score bars/readouts lilac (parameters), matching Night School token semantics throughout.
Every deliberate element carries content; the only questionable marks are faint leftover shapes in frame 3, which read as an incomplete render rather than decoration.

The promised instrument is on screen: amber probe, four periwinkle candidates, per-candidate lilac score bars with mono readouts, the probe sweeping while bars climb and die, and c1 stretched longer in the final frames.
Each score bar is placed in a column beneath its named candidate and each label hugs its arrow, keeping claims near their objects.
The sequence builds in reasoning order — fold-down to a point, then arrows, then bars, then sweep and stretch — reading as a continuous construction rather than cuts.
Frame 3 contains faint half-rendered ghost shapes (a dim square and dim text near center-left) beside the +2.5 chip, muddying the reading order and negative space.
suggestion: Remove or fully resolve the faint leftover chip/text elements in frame 3 so only the intended worked recipe chip is visible.
